response.justification: Bogdan is a seasoned polyglot engineer with strong TypeScript/Node.js expertise and broad software craftsmanship credentials, but he fundamentally lacks the core technical requirements for this role. Java appears only peripherally across his career (robotic arms tooling at Altom, C IDE work at National Instruments), Kotlin is entirely absent, and there is no evidence of experience with Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM enterprise framework. His developer tooling background (NI LabWindows, Stripe MCP generators) shows genuine interest in the tooling space, but it is C/C++ and TypeScript-based rather than JVM-focused, making the gap too wide for a mid-senior JVM IDE role.
strengths: Hands-on developer tooling experience across two roles (NI LabWindows C IDE components, Stripe MCP code generators and agentic frameworks)
Consistent software craftsmanship practices — TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD — demonstrated across multiple senior and staff-level positions
Proven ability to own complex, large-scale systems under real production load (Shopify direct mail at millions of sends, Sociomantic at millions of HTTP req/s)
concerns: No meaningful Java or Kotlin expertise; Java appears only as a secondary language in two older roles and Kotlin is entirely absent from the resume
Zero experience with Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM enterprise framework — the central technical focus of the position
Career trajectory is firmly in TypeScript/Node.js web and backend development, with no signal of JVM ecosystem investment or IDE internals work on the JVM side
